zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabel Gray於1365年出生在英國越可爾也達爾時，她的父親John de Grey Baron Grey20歲，母親Elizabeth de Poynings16歲。她與Robert Thornton, Jr.結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1448年11月3日在英國英格蘭越可爾 易亞斯特 利丁武特 牛頓享年83歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Isabel Gray于1365年出生在英国越可爾也達爾时，她的父亲John de Grey Baron Grey20岁，母亲Elizabeth de Poynings16岁。她与Robert Thornton, Jr.结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1448年11月3日在英国英格兰越可爾 易亞斯特 利丁武特 牛頓享年83岁。

nameMeanings.name: Gray
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish (especially Eastern Ulster; of Norman origin): habitational name from Graye in Calvados, France, named from the Gallo-Roman personal name Graec(i)us, meaning ‘Greek’ + the locative suffix -acum. This is probably the chief source of the surname in Britain.

English: nickname for someone with gray hair or a gray beard, from Middle English grey (Old English grǣg, grēg) ‘gray’. In Ireland it has been used as a translation of various Gaelic surnames derived from riabhach ‘brindled, gray’, including Mac Giolla Riabhaigh; see McGreevy . In North America, this surname has assimilated names with similar meaning from other languages.
French: habitational name from Gray in Haute-Saône or Le Gray in Seine-Maritime.

variants.name: McGreevy
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Seamus, Brendan.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mag Riabhaigh, a patronymic from the personal name Riabhach ‘brindled, grizzled’. Compare Reavey and Creevy 2.

variants.name: Grayer
meanings: English (southern): nickname from Middle English grei ‘gray’ (see Gray ) + her ‘hair’.
Scottish (Ayrshire): variant of   Greer  .

variants.name: Grays
meanings: English (Durham): variant of Gray , with post-medieval excrescent -s, or of Grace .

variants.name: Grice
meanings: English (Midlands): from Middle English grice, grise ‘pig’ (Old Norse gríss), probably a nickname or a metonymic occupational name for a swineherd.
English: nickname for a gray-haired man, from Middle English grice, gris ‘gray’ (Old French gris, apparently of ancient Germanic origin, and probably a distant cognate of Gray 1).
Possibly also an Americanized form of German   Greis  .

variants.name: Grey
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish: variant of   Gray  .
German: dialect variant of   Grau  .
In some cases also an American shortened and altered form of Ukrainian Grushko: nickname or topographic name derived from a Russian-influenced transliteration of grushka ‘pear, pear tree’.

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabel Gray was born in 1365, in Ryedale, Yorkshire, England, her father, John de Grey Baron Grey, was 20 and her mother, Elizabeth de Poynings, was 16. She married Robert Thornton, Jr.. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 3 November 1448, in Out Newton, Yorkshire East Riding, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 83.

ko.East Newton, East Riding of Yorkshire, E: 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 이스트 리딩 우트 뉴턴
ko.lifeSummary: Isabel Gray(이)가 1365년 영국 요르크실르 리들일에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John de Grey Baron Grey(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth de Poynings(은)는 16세였다. 그녀는 Robert Thornton, Jr.(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1448년 11월 3일 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 이스트 리딩 우트 뉴턴에서 83세의 나이로 사망했다.

Она умерла 3 ноября 1448 г. в местности оут нютон, еркшир эаст ридинг, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 83 лет.
